behavior_summary: U8P_SCREAMER::look: When the engine runs the object's U8P_SCREAMER::look/process handler (examine or related fast-area trigger), the object emits a single short bark 'screamer' and then suspends/ends the U8P_SCREAMER::look process.

U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit: Handles an incoming hit/target reference (param1). It treats the parameter as an item reference and branches based on whether the target is an NPC. If Item::isNpc(local1) is true then local2 is used as an NPC index/reference: when local2 == 0x01 a special-hit flow runs (play sound, apply Item::receiveHit using the NPC's HP, reset a kernel ref); otherwise the NPC is teleported to a random offset, marked dead, and several FREE/Kernel reference slots are cleared. If the target is not an NPC the code destroys the item.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SCREAMER::look: Straight-line behavior — single Bark node then suspend/return.
U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit: Primary branching on Item::isNpc(local1) (NPC vs non-NPC).

U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit: Secondary branch when NPC: compare local2 == 0x01 to choose between (a) special-hit handling (playSFX + Item::receiveHit + Kernel::resetRef) or (b) teleport + Npc::setDead + FREE::resetRef cleanup.
open_questions: Who/what invokes U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit and under which in-game events is it called (projectile hit, scripted effect, trap)? (confidence: medium)
What is the semantic meaning of the numeric literal 0x01 compared against local2 (e.g., a specific NPC index, a flagged state, or a particular handler id)? (confidence: low)
What exactly do the numeric flags passed to Item::receiveHit (0x0080) and the various ref-slot ids (0x00F0, 0x0200, 0x0205) denote in runtime bookkeeping? (confidence: low)
Is U8P_SCREAMER::look also invoked by entering a fast-area trigger in addition to explicit player 'U8P_SCREAMER::look' actions, or is it strictly the player's examine action? (confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_SCREAMER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the player-facing short description when the object/NPC is examined (emit a single bark 'screamer').
entry_conditions: Engine 'U8P_SCREAMER::look' / inspect action targeting this object (process type 0x0208).
Inferred: possibly also triggered by entering the object's fast-area (common engine behavior for U8P_SCREAMER::look-type processes).

function_map.name: U8P_SCREAMER::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_SCREAMER::look handler that emits a single bark

function_map.name: U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit
function_map.role: utility process to handle incoming hit/target references and apply NPC vs non-NPC effects (damage, teleport, death, or destruction)

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether the passed reference param1/local1 is an NPC to select NPC-specific handling.
Play a specific sound effect in the special-hit branch (called with 0x00C8, 0x5C).
Obtain the NPC's current HP to pass as the damage/HP argument into Item::receiveHit in the special-hit flow.
Apply a hit/damage to the NPC/item (special-hit path uses flags 0x0080 and the NPC's HP).
Reset/clear a kernel-level reference slot after special-hit handling (slot 0x00F0, using local2).
Teleport the NPC to a new location using random offsets (non-special NPC branch).
Mark the teleported/handled NPC as dead in the non-special branch.
Clear/free bookkeeping references for the NPC (observed slot ids: 0x0200, 0x00F0, 0x0205) during cleanup.
Destroy or remove the target when the passed parameter does not refer to an NPC.
Generate random offsets (max 0x2710) used for NPC teleport coordinates.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SCREAMER::look: When the engine runs the object's U8P_SCREAMER::look/process handler (examine or related fast-area trigger), the object emits a single short bark 'screamer' and then suspends/ends the U8P_SCREAMER::look process.
